subject: Physics
title: Does Light Experience Time?
description: A fast breakdown of Does Light Experience Time? for builders.

hook.text: From a photon's perspective, no time passes at all. It is born and absorbed in the same instant.

buildup.text: Special relativity says time slows as you approach the speed of light. At light speed, time stops.

discovery.text: A photon from a star 100 million light-years away experiences zero travel time from its own frame.

twist.text: Technically, photons don't have a valid rest frame. The question is almost illegal in physics.

climax.text: Still, the implication is haunting: light lives in a frozen moment that spans the entire universe.

punchline.text: For light, the universe is instant. Time is for everything else.

quiz.question: What does special relativity imply about a photon's experience of time?
options: No time passes for a photon
